title.en: Carrying Key Management Over 802.15.4: Overview, Vocabulary, and the MPX Data Service
title.zh: 在 802.15.4 上承载密钥管理：标准概览、术语表与 MPX 数据服务

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802.15.9-2021 fills a gap left open by IEEE Std 802.15.4, the standard for low-rate wireless personal area networks (WPANs — short-range, low-power radio networks like those used by smart-home sensors). 802.15.4 can encrypt and authenticate frames, but it never says how the two ends agree on the secret keys. Before 802.15.9, every vendor invented its own incompatible scheme. This standard defines an interoperable way to carry a key management protocol (KMP — a set of message exchanges that generate, distribute, and replace cryptographic keys) across an 802.15.4 link. Clause 1.1 (General) states the goal in one line: a standard for transporting KMP datagrams for WPANs.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802.15.9-2021 填补了 IEEE Std 802.15.4（低速率无线个域网标准）留下的一个空白。WPAN（无线个域网）是指智能家居传感器等使用的短距离、低功耗无线网络。802.15.4 能够对帧进行加密和认证，却从未规定两端如何就密钥达成一致。在 802.15.9 之前，各厂商各自发明互不兼容的方案。本标准定义了一种可互操作的方式，用以在 802.15.4 链路上承载密钥管理协议（KMP——一组用于生成、分发和更换密钥的消息交换）。第 1.1 节（General，总则）用一句话点明目标：为 WPAN 制定承载 KMP 数据报的标准。

blocks.en: The opening clauses set the boundaries. Clause 1.2 (Scope) says the standard adds key management extensions: session-key generation at both 128-bit and 256-bit lengths, creation and transport of broadcast/multicast keys, and algorithm agility (the ability to negotiate which cipher to use) — while staying backwards-compatible with the 2016 edition. Clause 1.3 (Purpose) explains the headline mechanism: a general-purpose multiplexed (MPX) data service that fragments, reassembles, and dispatches payloads too large to fit in a single media access control (MAC — the sublayer that controls frame transmission) frame. Clause 1.4 (Deprecated features) retires one option: the PANA-based KMP described in Annex D is deprecated. Clause 1.5 (Word usage) defines the famous keyword ladder — shall (mandatory), should (recommended), may (permitted), and can (capability) — so every later sentence reads precisely.
blocks.zh: 开篇各节划定了边界。第 1.2 节（Scope，范围）指出本标准新增的密钥管理扩展：128 位和 256 位两种长度的会话密钥生成、广播/组播密钥的创建与传输，以及算法敏捷性（即协商使用何种密码算法的能力）——同时保持与 2016 版的向后兼容。第 1.3 节（Purpose，目的）阐明核心机制：一个通用的多路复用（MPX）数据服务，对那些大到无法装进单个 MAC（媒体访问控制——负责帧传输的子层）帧的载荷进行分片、重组与分派。第 1.4 节（Deprecated features，弃用特性）淘汰了一个选项：附录 D 中描述的基于 PANA 的 KMP 被弃用。第 1.5 节（Word usage，用词规范）定义了著名的关键词层级——shall（强制）、should（推荐）、may（允许）、can（能力）——使后文每句话都精确无歧义。

blocks.en: Clause 3 builds the shared vocabulary. Clause 3.1 (Definitions) keeps only two terms local: an extended address (the 64-bit unique identifier from 802.15.4) and the key management protocol (KMP) itself — defined as a collection of data transactions that generate, exchange, store, use, and replace cryptographic keys. Clause 3.2 (Acronyms and abbreviations) is a dense glossary; the ones you will meet most often are MPX (multiplexed), KMP (key management protocol), MAC (media access control), SA (security association — the bundle of keys and parameters two devices share), PIB (PAN information base — the device's configuration database), IE (information element — a typed field inside a MAC frame), and PAN (personal area network).
blocks.zh: 第 3 节构建共享词汇。第 3.1 节（Definitions，定义）仅在本地定义两个术语：extended address（扩展地址，即 802.15.4 中的 64 位唯一标识符）和 key management protocol（KMP）本身——定义为一组用于生成、交换、存储、使用和更换密钥的数据事务。第 3.2 节（Acronyms and abbreviations，缩略语）是一张密集的术语表；你最常遇到的是 MPX（多路复用）、KMP（密钥管理协议）、MAC（媒体访问控制）、SA（security association，安全关联——两台设备共享的密钥与参数集合）、PIB（PAN 信息库——设备的配置数据库）、IE（information element，信息元素——MAC 帧内一个带类型的字段）以及 PAN（个域网）。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 460 250" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="40" y="10" width="380" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="32" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">Next higher layer (KMP config)</text><rect x="40" y="58" width="180" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="130" y="80" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">KMP service</text><rect x="240" y="58" width="180" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="330" y="80" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Other MPX users</text><line x1="130" y1="92" x2="130" y2="110"/><line x1="330" y1="92" x2="330" y2="110"/><rect x="40" y="110" width="380" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="132" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">MPX data service (fragment / multiplex)</text><line x1="230" y1="144" x2="230" y2="162"/><rect x="40" y="162" width="380" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="184" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">MAC services (data + management)</text><line x1="230" y1="196" x2="230" y2="214"/><rect x="40" y="214" width="380" height="30" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="233" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">PHY services</text></svg>

caption.en: Clause 4.2 system view (Figure 1 of the standard): the MPX data service sits between the MAC and the higher layers. The KMP service and other MPX users feed payloads down through the MPX-SAP; MPX fragments and multiplexes them onto MAC data frames.
caption.zh: 第 4.2 节系统视图（标准中的图 1）：MPX 数据服务位于 MAC 与上层之间。KMP 服务和其他 MPX 用户通过 MPX-SAP 将载荷向下传递；MPX 对其进行分片并多路复用到 MAC 数据帧上。

blocks.en: Clause 4 (Introduction) paints the big picture in nine short subclauses. Clause 4.1 (Overview) restates why this standard exists — 802.15.4 secures datagrams but leaves key management unspecified — and lists the KMPs it gives guidance for: HIP (host identity protocol), IKEv2 (Internet Key Exchange version 2), PANA, Dragonfly, and 802.1X. Clause 4.2 (System view) places MPX between MAC services and the higher layers (the figure above). Clause 4.3 (Network view) sets a key constraint: fragmentation and reassembly operate hop by hop between two direct peers; if every KMP payload already fits in one MAC protocol data unit (MPDU), the fragmentation machinery simply never engages. Clause 4.4 (Security associations) explains that a KMP's job is to set up and maintain an SA — which may be unicast or group — whose keys land in the 802.15.4 security PIB.
blocks.zh: 第 4 节（Introduction，引言）用九个简短的子节勾勒全貌。第 4.1 节（Overview，概述）重申本标准存在的理由——802.15.4 保护数据报，却未规定密钥管理——并列出它提供指导的各种 KMP：HIP（主机身份协议）、IKEv2（互联网密钥交换第 2 版）、PANA、Dragonfly 和 802.1X。第 4.2 节（System view，系统视图）将 MPX 置于 MAC 服务与上层之间（见上图）。第 4.3 节（Network view，网络视图）给出一个关键约束：分片与重组在两个直接对等端之间逐跳进行；若每个 KMP 载荷本就能装进一个 MPDU（MAC 协议数据单元），分片机制根本不会启动。第 4.4 节（Security associations，安全关联）说明 KMP 的职责是建立并维护一个 SA（可为单播或组播），其密钥最终落入 802.15.4 安全 PIB。

blocks.en: The remaining Clause 4 subclauses cover triggering, structure, and limits. Clause 4.5 (Process flow) describes when an SA gets established: in a beaconing PAN either device may start the KMP; in a non-beaconing PAN, a sender requiring security starts the KMP first, and a receiver that gets unsecured data simply drops it and starts the KMP itself. Clause 4.6 (State machine) notes there are separate inbound and outbound processing state machines (detailed later in Clause 9). Clause 4.7 (Address formats) requires both ends to know each other's 64-bit extended address before any link key can be set up — the SA is bound to that address. Clause 4.8 (KMP payload size) gives the numbers: MPX allows up to 256 fragments, and since the Total Upper Layer Frame Size field is two octets, the maximum KMP payload is 65 535 octets. Clause 4.9 (Format conventions) simply inherits the formatting rules of IEEE Std 802.15.4-2020.
blocks.zh: 第 4 节其余子节涵盖触发、结构与上限。第 4.5 节（Process flow，流程）描述 SA 何时建立：在有信标的 PAN 中任一设备均可发起 KMP；在无信标的 PAN 中，要求安全的发送方先发起 KMP，而收到非安全数据的接收方则直接丢弃并自行发起 KMP。第 4.6 节（State machine，状态机）指出存在独立的入向与出向处理状态机（细节见后文第 9 节）。第 4.7 节（Address formats，地址格式）要求在建立任何链路密钥之前，两端都须知晓对方的 64 位扩展地址——SA 即绑定到该地址。第 4.8 节（KMP payload size，KMP 载荷大小）给出数字：MPX 最多允许 256 个分片，又因 Total Upper Layer Frame Size 字段为两个八位组，KMP 载荷最大为 65 535 个八位组。第 4.9 节（Format conventions，格式约定）直接沿用 IEEE Std 802.15.4-2020 的格式规则。

blocks.en: Clause 5 specifies the MPX data service itself. Clause 5.1 (Description) explains the core idea: MPX carries payloads from several higher-layer services between two MAC entities, fragmenting a large payload into a sequence of MAC frames at the originator and reassembling it at the recipient. It leans on the MAC acknowledgment (ACK) so the originator knows each fragment arrived, and the MAC handles retransmission. A few hard rules appear here: each MPX IE Content field is capped at macMpxMaxFragmentSize octets; a NO_ACK failure aborts the transaction and returns an error upward; and because the MAC often cannot signal failure cleanly, the recipient runs a macMpxReassemblyTimeout timer to give up on a stalled transfer. An ACK only proves the fragment reached the recipient's MAC — not that reassembly succeeded.
blocks.zh: 第 5 节规定 MPX 数据服务本身。第 5.1 节（Description，描述）阐明核心思想：MPX 在两个 MAC 实体之间承载来自多个上层服务的载荷，在发起方将大载荷分片成一串 MAC 帧，在接收方重组。它依赖 MAC 确认（ACK）让发起方确认每个分片已送达，重传由 MAC 负责。这里出现了几条硬性规则：每个 MPX IE 内容字段上限为 macMpxMaxFragmentSize 个八位组；NO_ACK 失败会中止事务并向上层返回错误；又因 MAC 往往无法干净地发出失败信号，接收方运行 macMpxReassemblyTimeout 计时器以放弃停滞的传输。ACK 仅证明分片到达了接收方的 MAC——并不表示重组已成功。

blocks.en:
sequenceDiagram
  participant NH1 as Originator higher layer
  participant O as Originator MPX/MAC
  participant R as Recipient MPX/MAC
  participant NH2 as Recipient higher layer
  NH1->>O: MPX-DATA.request
  O->>R: Fragment 1 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  O->>R: Fragment 2 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  O->>R: Fragment 3 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  R->>NH2: MPX-DATA.indication (reassembled)
  O->>NH1: MPX-DATA.confirm (SUCCESS)
blocks.zh:
sequenceDiagram
  participant NH1 as 发起方上层
  participant O as 发起方 MPX/MAC
  participant R as 接收方 MPX/MAC
  participant NH2 as 接收方上层
  NH1->>O: MPX-DATA.request
  O->>R: 分片 1 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  O->>R: 分片 2 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  O->>R: 分片 3 (Data)
  R-->>O: ACK
  R->>NH2: MPX-DATA.indication (已重组)
  O->>NH1: MPX-DATA.confirm (SUCCESS)

blocks.en: Clause 5.2 (MPX data primitives) defines the four service calls a higher layer uses, summarized in the standard's Table 1. Clause 5.2.1 (MPX data primitive use) walks through the message sequences — a clean three-fragment transfer (above), a failed transfer that ends in NO_ACK with an optional abort IE, an aborted transfer where the recipient lacks resources, and an immediate refusal carried back inside an Enhanced-Ack. Clause 5.2.2 (MPX-DATA.request) is the downward call that asks MPX to send a payload; its parameters include source/destination addressing modes, DstPanId, MultiplexId (which higher-layer protocol owns this data), the MpxData octets, an MpxHandle to match the later confirm, and the 802.15.4 security parameters (SecurityLevel, KeyIdMode, KeySource, KeyIndex). Clause 5.2.3 (MPX-DATA.confirm) reports the outcome — SUCCESS or TRANSACTION_ABORTED — and a MaxTransferSize the peer may suggest. Clause 5.2.4 (MPX-DATA.indication) is the upward delivery of a fully reassembled payload to the recipient's higher layer.
blocks.zh: 第 5.2 节（MPX data primitives，MPX 数据原语）定义上层使用的四个服务调用，汇总于标准的表 1。第 5.2.1 节（MPX data primitive use，MPX 数据原语用法）逐一讲解消息时序——一次干净的三分片传输（见上图）、一次以 NO_ACK 结束并可选携带中止 IE 的失败传输、一次接收方资源不足而中止的传输，以及一次通过增强确认（Enhanced-Ack）即时回送的拒绝。第 5.2.2 节（MPX-DATA.request）是请求 MPX 发送载荷的下行调用，其参数包括源/目的寻址模式、DstPanId、MultiplexId（标识哪个上层协议拥有此数据）、MpxData 八位组、用于匹配后续 confirm 的 MpxHandle，以及 802.15.4 安全参数（SecurityLevel、KeyIdMode、KeySource、KeyIndex）。第 5.2.3 节（MPX-DATA.confirm）报告结果——SUCCESS 或 TRANSACTION_ABORTED——以及对端可能建议的 MaxTransferSize。第 5.2.4 节（MPX-DATA.indication）则向接收方上层上行交付完整重组后的载荷。

blocks.en: Notice the asymmetry in Table 1: MPX-DATA has request, indication, and confirm but no response, while MPX-PURGE has only request and confirm. The MpxHandle is the thread that ties a confirm back to the request that started it — keep an eye on it as you read the primitive tables.
blocks.zh: 留意表 1 中的不对称：MPX-DATA 有 request、indication 和 confirm，却没有 response；而 MPX-PURGE 只有 request 和 confirm。MpxHandle 是把 confirm 关联回发起它的 request 的那根线索——阅读原语表时请始终盯住它。

blocks.en: Clause 5.3 (MPX-PURGE primitive) lets a higher layer cancel a pending transfer. Clause 5.3.1 (MPX-PURGE primitive use) shows the idea with an example sequence: the originator decides to drop a transaction still sitting in its queue. Clause 5.3.2 (MPX-PURGE.request) takes an MpxHandle and a SendAbort flag — if the matching payload is still in the transaction queue it is discarded, and if SendAbort is TRUE an abort message (an MPX IE with Transfer type 0b110) is sent so the peer can clear its state immediately instead of waiting for the timeout; if an MCPS-DATA.request is already in flight, MPX issues a matching MCPS-PURGE.request to the MAC. Clause 5.3.3 (MPX-PURGE.confirm) reports SUCCESS, or INVALID_HANDLE if the payload had already left the queue and could no longer be purged.
blocks.zh: 第 5.3 节（MPX-PURGE primitive，MPX-PURGE 原语）让上层取消一个待处理的传输。第 5.3.1 节（MPX-PURGE primitive use，用法）用一个示例时序说明思路：发起方决定丢弃仍在其队列中的某个事务。第 5.3.2 节（MPX-PURGE.request）接收一个 MpxHandle 和一个 SendAbort 标志——若匹配的载荷仍在事务队列中则将其丢弃；若 SendAbort 为 TRUE，则发送一条中止消息（Transfer type 为 0b110 的 MPX IE），使对端可立即清除状态而无需等待超时；若已有 MCPS-DATA.request 在传输中，MPX 会向 MAC 发出相应的 MCPS-PURGE.request。第 5.3.3 节（MPX-PURGE.confirm）报告 SUCCESS；若载荷已离开队列、无法再被清除，则报告 INVALID_HANDLE。

blocks.en: Clause 5.4 (MPX PIB attributes) closes the clause with the two knobs that tune the service, stored in the device's PIB (PAN information base) and readable or writable by the higher layer through MLME-GET / MLME-SET. The standard's Table 7 lists them: macMpxReassemblyTimeout (range 0–65 535 seconds, default 30) — how long the recipient waits before discarding partially reassembled frames — and macMpxMaxFragmentSize (range 7–2047, default 96 octets) — the maximum MPX IE Content size when fragmenting. Those two defaults are exactly why Clause 4.8's worked example assumed a 96-octet effective fragment.
blocks.zh: 第 5.4 节（MPX PIB attributes，MPX PIB 属性）以两个调节服务的旋钮收尾，它们存于设备的 PIB（PAN 信息库），上层可通过 MLME-GET / MLME-SET 读写。标准的表 7 列出二者：macMpxReassemblyTimeout（范围 0–65 535 秒，默认 30）——接收方丢弃部分重组帧前的等待时长——以及 macMpxMaxFragmentSize（范围 7–2047，默认 96 个八位组）——分片时 MPX IE 内容的最大尺寸。正是这两个默认值，使第 4.8 节的算例采用了 96 八位组的有效分片大小。

summary.en: Coverage map — every clause this lesson teaches, by number
summary.zh: 覆盖地图——本课讲授的每个条款（按编号）

blocks.en: Clause 1 Overview: 1.1 General (a standard for transporting KMP datagrams for WPANs); 1.2 Scope (128/256-bit session keys, broadcast/multicast keys, algorithm agility, backwards-compatible with 2016); 1.3 Purpose (the MPX data service: fragmentation, reassembly, protocol dispatch); 1.4 Deprecated features (PANA KMP of Annex D deprecated); 1.5 Word usage (shall/should/may/can). Clause 3: 3.1 Definitions (extended address; KMP); 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations (MPX, KMP, MAC, SA, PIB, IE, PAN, HIP, IKEv2, PANA, EAPOL, MPDU, MSDU, and the full list). Clause 4 Introduction: 4.1 Overview (why the standard exists; KMPs it guides — HIP, IKEv2, PANA, Dragonfly, 802.1X); 4.2 System view (MPX between MAC and higher layers, Figure 1); 4.3 Network view (hop-by-hop fragmentation between direct peers; skipped when a payload fits one MPDU); 4.4 Security associations (KMP sets up/maintains an SA, unicast or group, keys to the 802.15.4 PIB); 4.5 Process flow (beaconing vs non-beaconing triggers); 4.6 State machine (separate inbound/outbound, see Clause 9); 4.7 Address formats (both ends need the 64-bit extended address); 4.8 KMP payload size (up to 256 fragments; max 65 535 octets via 2-octet size field); 4.9 Format conventions (inherit 802.15.4-2020). Clause 5 MPX data service: 5.1 Description (fragment/reassemble over MAC ACK; macMpxMaxFragmentSize cap; NO_ACK abort; reassembly timeout); 5.2.1 primitive use (success/failed/aborted/Enh-Ack sequences, Table 1); 5.2.2 MPX-DATA.request (Table 2 parameters); 5.2.3 MPX-DATA.confirm (SUCCESS / TRANSACTION_ABORTED, MaxTransferSize); 5.2.4 MPX-DATA.indication (reassembled delivery); 5.3.1 MPX-PURGE use (Figure 6); 5.3.2 MPX-PURGE.request (MpxHandle, SendAbort, abort IE 0b110, MCPS-PURGE); 5.3.3 MPX-PURGE.confirm (SUCCESS / INVALID_HANDLE); 5.4 MPX PIB attributes (macMpxReassemblyTimeout default 30 s; macMpxMaxFragmentSize default 96, Table 7).
blocks.zh: 第 1 节 概览：1.1 总则（为 WPAN 承载 KMP 数据报的标准）；1.2 范围（128/256 位会话密钥、广播/组播密钥、算法敏捷性、向后兼容 2016 版）；1.3 目的（MPX 数据服务：分片、重组、协议分派）；1.4 弃用特性（弃用附录 D 的 PANA KMP）；1.5 用词规范（shall/should/may/can）。第 3 节：3.1 定义（扩展地址；KMP）；3.2 缩略语（MPX、KMP、MAC、SA、PIB、IE、PAN、HIP、IKEv2、PANA、EAPOL、MPDU、MSDU 等完整列表）。第 4 节 引言：4.1 概述（标准存在的理由；其指导的 KMP——HIP、IKEv2、PANA、Dragonfly、802.1X）；4.2 系统视图（MPX 位于 MAC 与上层之间，图 1）；4.3 网络视图（直接对等端间逐跳分片；载荷可装入一个 MPDU 时跳过）；4.4 安全关联（KMP 建立/维护 SA，单播或组播，密钥写入 802.15.4 PIB）；4.5 流程（有信标与无信标的触发）；4.6 状态机（独立的入向/出向，见第 9 节）；4.7 地址格式（两端需 64 位扩展地址）；4.8 KMP 载荷大小（最多 256 分片；经 2 八位组的尺寸字段最大 65 535 八位组）；4.9 格式约定（沿用 802.15.4-2020）。第 5 节 MPX 数据服务：5.1 描述（基于 MAC ACK 分片/重组；macMpxMaxFragmentSize 上限；NO_ACK 中止；重组超时）；5.2.1 原语用法（成功/失败/中止/增强确认时序，表 1）；5.2.2 MPX-DATA.request（表 2 参数）；5.2.3 MPX-DATA.confirm（SUCCESS / TRANSACTION_ABORTED、MaxTransferSize）；5.2.4 MPX-DATA.indication（重组后交付）；5.3.1 MPX-PURGE 用法（图 6）；5.3.2 MPX-PURGE.request（MpxHandle、SendAbort、中止 IE 0b110、MCPS-PURGE）；5.3.3 MPX-PURGE.confirm（SUCCESS / INVALID_HANDLE）；5.4 MPX PIB 属性（macMpxReassemblyTimeout 默认 30 秒；macMpxMaxFragmentSize 默认 96，表 7）。

cites.note: Clause 1 Overview (1.1-1.5), Clause 3 definitions and acronyms (3.1-3.2)
Clause 4 Introduction (4.1-4.9) including Figure 1 system view; Clause 5.1 Description
Clause 5.2 MPX data primitives (5.2.1 use + sequence figures, 5.2.2 request, 5.2.3 confirm, 5.2.4 indication)
Clause 5.3 MPX-PURGE (5.3.1-5.3.3) and Clause 5.4 MPX PIB attributes (Table 7)
